content.problemStatement:
A set of integers is called good if there does not exist three distinct elements a, b, c in it such that a + b = c.

Your task is simple. Just output any good set of **n** integers. All the elements in this set should be distinct and should lie between 1 and 500, both inclusive.
constraints: All output numbers must be between 1 and 500 inclusive
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T (1 ≤ T ≤ 100), the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer n (1 ≤ n ≤ 100), the required size of a good set.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a line with n distinct integers between 1 and 500 (inclusive) such that no three distinct elements a, b, c satisfy a + b = c. The numbers may be printed in any order and must be separated by a single space.

sampleTestCases.explanation: For n=1 we output the first number 251. For n=3 we output 251,252,253. For n=5 we output 251‑255. All numbers are >250, so any sum of two distinct numbers exceeds 500 and cannot be present in the set, guaranteeing a good set.

solution.approach: Observe that if all chosen numbers are larger than 250, the sum of any two distinct numbers is at least 251+252 = 503, which is greater than the maximum allowed value 500. Therefore such a sum can never be another element of the set. Hence, for any n we can simply output the first n integers starting from 251 (i.e., 251, 252, …). This construction is O(n) per test case and always produces a valid good set because all elements are distinct and lie in the required range.
solution.timeComplexity: O(n) per test case, where n is the size of the required set. Overall O(T·n).
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) extra space besides the output.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    it = iter(data)
    T = int(next(it))
    out_lines = []
    for _ in range(T):
        n = int(next(it))
        line = ' '.join(str(251 + i) for i in range(n))
        out_lines.append(line)
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out_lines))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
